_metadata.description: P1.6 omega/fish-oil module rubric. Defines dimension caps, per-dimension sub-component weights, IFOS scope policy, EPA/DHA dose bands, and form/source/sustainability/oxidation scoring rules. Consumed by scripts/scoring_v4/modules/omega.py and its per-dimension helpers.
_metadata.notes: Config-driven per Codex's P1.5 omega-debt plan + Sean's 2026-05-20 instruction. Every weight here must be referenced by code AND locked by tests (no untested policy blob). Backed by scoring_config.section_A_ingredient_quality.omega3_dose_bonus for the dose-band heritage.

formulation._doc: Form tier + Source disclosed + Premium-form A2 carryforward + EPA/DHA concentration + Marine sustainability cert (FoS/MSC). Oxidation testing lives in Transparency, not here (per Sean 2026-05-20).

form_tier._doc: Molecular form of EPA/DHA in the product. TG and rTG are top-tier triglyceride structures; rTG supports high-concentration premium fish oil while preserving triglyceride structure. PL = phospholipid form (krill — high bioavailability via phosphatidylcholine carrier). EE = ethyl ester (cheaper, lower bioavailability for some markers). undefined = label does not disclose form.

source_disclosed._doc: Product label or ingredient panel discloses the marine source (fish / cod liver / krill / algae). Boolean trigger — +4 when any of these source keywords appears in product_name or ingredient_quality_data[].name.

premium_form_a2_carry._doc: EPA/DHA labeled AND molecular form labeled together — carries v3's A2 premium-delivery credit forward into the omega module. Only awarded when form_tier != 'undefined'.

epa_dha_concentration._doc: EPA+DHA concentration as a proportion of disclosed parent omega oil mass. Requires label-disclosed EPA/DHA mg and parent oil mg; no product-name inference. Rewards concentrated fish/algae/krill oils without overcrediting products that omit oil mass.

sustainability_cert._doc: Marine sustainability cert (Friend of the Sea / MSC) — verified via rules_db third_party_programs.verified=true. Per Codex §9: 'lower weight than purity/testing certs' — sits in Formulation as a product attribute, not Trust as a third-party-test signal.
sustainability_cert._eligibility_doc: Required: program present in certification_data.evidence_based.third_party_programs with score_eligible=true. Bare label-text without rules_db backing does NOT score (per 'no manufacturer overcredit' policy).

dose._doc: EPA+DHA per-day dose adequacy bands (lifted and rescaled from scoring_config.section_A_ingredient_quality.omega3_dose_bonus.bands) + EPA:DHA ratio sanity bonus. Rescaled cap is /20 (band) + /5 (ratio) = 25.

epa_dha_bands.label: high_clinical
epa_dha_bands._source: EFSA approved health claim for blood triglycerides: >= 2 g/day

epa_dha_bands.label: aha_cvd
epa_dha_bands._source: AHA recommendation for CVD patients: ~1 g/day EPA+DHA

epa_dha_bands.label: general_health
epa_dha_bands._source: FDA qualified health claim minimum for combined EPA+DHA

epa_dha_bands.label: efsa_ai_zone
epa_dha_bands._source: EFSA (2012) AI for EPA+DHA: 250 mg/day general population

ratio_sanity._doc: EPA:DHA ratio falling in a healthy range (between 1:3 and 3:1) earns +5. Outside that range — extreme imbalance for a marketed EPA+DHA product — earns 0. Pure DHA (algal) or pure EPA products are exempt: if either EPA or DHA is zero, ratio sanity is skipped (returns 0, not penalized).
ratio_sanity._ratio_doc: ratio = EPA_mg / DHA_mg. 0.333 = 1:3 (DHA-dominant). 3.0 = 3:1 (EPA-dominant). Most clinical evidence supports ratios in this range; outside this either over-concentrates one fatty acid (suggesting non-standard formulation) or implies an inactive companion that wasn't disclosed.

evidence._doc: Delegates to the generic evidence pipeline with omega-specific canonicals (EPA, DHA) prioritized in top-N selection. EPA/DHA evidence is well-established AHA/EFSA-backed; cap stays /20 with cap_per_ingredient = 7 inherited from scoring_config.

disclosed_epa_dha_clinical_floor._doc: Conservative class-evidence floor when a product discloses EPA/DHA at or above the EFSA adequate-intake zone. Prevents well-disclosed EPA/DHA products from scoring as evidence-poor solely because the generic evidence matcher emits one broad omega-3 match. Parent fish-oil mass without EPA/DHA disclosure does not qualify.

indication_relevance._doc: Bonus for products dosed at an indication-relevant omega threshold. Score 5 if EPA+DHA >= 1000 mg/day (AHA CVD threshold) OR if a prenatal DHA-labeled product meets the prenatal DHA target already used by omega_dose.

trust._doc: Per Sean 2026-05-20: only sku and curated product_line scopes score IFOS. needs_review and brand_only stay 0 to preserve P0.1b/P1.7 cleanup discipline. Uncertainty resolves via P1.7 curated overrides, NOT partial credit.
trust._b4a_policy_rationale: IFOS is omega-specific, but it is still product/lot/registry evidence, not generic brand reputation. If a registry match is real, curate it into product_line via the P1.7.3 review CLI. If it is not product-specific, it can appear in display or manufacturer trust, not product Trust.

b4b_gmp._doc: B4b GMP credit. nsf_gmp (NSF/ANSI 173 facility audit) = 4. fda_registered = 2. self-attested gmp.claimed without nsf/fda backing = 0 (per P1.8 enricher hardening).

b4c_traceability._doc: B4c lot-level traceability: 1 point total when EITHER has_coa OR has_batch_lookup (including nested QR-code rollup per P1.8b).

transparency._doc: EPA/DHA breakdown disclosed (at least one component) + Form disclosed + Source disclosed + Oxidation testing disclosed + B3 claim_compliance bonus. Penalties B2 allergen / B5 opacity (class-aware) / B6 marketing reuse the same helpers as generic/probiotic.
transparency._epa_or_dha_disclosed_doc: Awarded when at least one of EPA or DHA is labeled with a positive mg quantity. Pure-EPA and pure-DHA (e.g. algal DHA) products qualify — disclosure of the active component(s) the product carries is what matters.

oxidation_disclosed._doc: Oxidation/TOTOX/peroxide/anisidine testing disclosed on the label or in certification_data. Future-ready: most products today emit 0 because DSLD labels rarely surface lot oxidation values. When P2 lot-test scrapers land, this signal will populate.

b3_claim_compliance._doc: Reuses generic Transparency B3 sub-rubric (allergen_free / gluten_free / vegan / vegetarian display claims), cap 4.

router._doc: Routing signals (any one triggers omega module dispatch). Inspected by scripts/scoring_v4/router.py with priority between multi_or_prenatal and generic. The ingredient-panel signal is the strongest because it operates on the enricher's canonicalized identity rather than label text.
router._standalone_word_boundary_doc: Word-boundary regex match for standalone EPA or DHA in product/brand/bundle name text. \b is critical to exclude false-positives like DHEA (D-H-E-A is one word; \bDHA\b does NOT match inside DHEA). Substring matching is forbidden for these short tokens.
router._ingredient_panel_doc: Strongest router signal: an enriched product whose ingredient_quality_data has an ingredient with canonical_id in {epa, dha, epa_dha} and a positive quantity routes to omega regardless of name keywords. Operates on the enricher's identity output, not loose text.
router._category_breakdown_removed_doc: A category_breakdown fatty_acid plurality check was REMOVED 2026-05-20 after a real-catalog audit caught ~250 false positives. The plurality signal flagged CLA / Borage Oil GLA / Flax Seed Oil ALA-only / MCT / Liposomal Glutathione (lecithin) as omega because all are fatty_acid in the enricher's category breakdown, but NONE bear EPA/DHA. Per the dev's 2026-05-20 RDA-table feedback: ALA is alpha-linolenic acid, a different molecule from EPA/DHA. ALA-only products belong in the generic module (where ALA gets the IOM AI when rda_optimal_uls is fixed). The plurality check was redundant — every legitimate omega product is already caught by ingredient_panel_canonicals.

completeness_gate._doc: Layer 2 completeness gate adds an omega-specific check: at least one EPA or DHA ingredient with quantity > 0 must be disclosed. Fish-oil-parent-only products (1000 mg fish oil, no EPA/DHA breakdown) fail and route to NOT_SCORED. Pure-EPA and pure-DHA products (algal DHA, prescription-grade pure EPA) DO qualify — the gate is 'at least one', not 'both'. Aligns with §9 'fish oil 1000 mg with no EPA/DHA breakdown should score significantly lower' rule, but enforced at the live-eligibility gate rather than as a score cap.
completeness_gate._eligibility_policy: At least one of canonical_id ∈ {epa, dha, epa_dha} with positive quantity. Requiring both globally would wrongly disqualify legitimate pure-component products.
